req.title: Fraud alert response (idempotency variant)
req.statement: A customer shall confirm or deny a suspected-fraud alert within 24 hours; unacknowledged alerts shall auto-freeze the account. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-088 has four distinct testable behaviours: (1) normal confirm/deny closes the alert and optionally reissues the card [positive, EP/STC], (2) duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key returns the original response without side-effects [positive, EP/DT], (3) responding to an already-expired (>24 h) alert returns 410 [negative, NEG], and (4) the system transitions to auto-freeze exactly at the 24-hour boundary when the alert is unacknowledged [boundary, BVA/STC]. These four cases together cover equivalence partitions (valid alert, duplicate key, expired alert), a state transition (active -> frozen), and the critical 24-hour boundary. Each test uses dedicated fixtures to remain self-contained and idempotent.

tests.name: test_confirm_fraud_alert_closes_alert_and_reissues_card
preconditions: A fraud alert with status=active exists for a known customer
The alert was created less than 24 hours ago
actions: PATCH /alerts/{id} with body {"response": "confirm"} and header Idempotency-Key: <unique_key>
expected: Alert status is closed when fetched via GET /alerts/{id}
trace: R-FIN-088: customer confirms fraud -> alert closed, card reissued
tests.executable:
def test_confirm_fraud_alert_closes_alert_and_reissues_card(api_client, active_fraud_alert, unique_idempotency_key):
    alert_id = active_fraud_alert["id"]
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": unique_idempotency_key}
    payload = {"response": "confirm"}

    resp = api_client.patch(f"/alerts/{alert_id}", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "closed"
    assert body["outcome"] == "fraud_confirmed"
    assert body.get("card_reissue_requested") is True

    get_resp = api_client.get(f"/alerts/{alert_id}")
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()["status"] == "closed"

tests.name: test_idempotent_duplicate_submission_returns_original_result
preconditions: A fraud alert with status=active exists for a known customer
The alert was created less than 24 hours ago
A fixed Idempotency-Key is available to be reused
actions: PATCH /alerts/{id} with body {"response": "deny"} and header Idempotency-Key: <fixed_key> (first call)
PATCH /alerts/{id} with identical body and identical Idempotency-Key (second call)
expected: Second response body is identical to first response body
No duplicate card reissue or side-effect is triggered
Alert status remains closed after the second call
trace: R-FIN-088: duplicate submission with same Idempotency-Key must return original result
tests.executable:
def test_idempotent_duplicate_submission_returns_original_result(api_client, active_fraud_alert):
    alert_id = active_fraud_alert["id"]
    fixed_key = "idem-key-deny-001"
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": fixed_key}
    payload = {"response": "deny"}

    resp1 = api_client.patch(f"/alerts/{alert_id}", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp1.status_code == 200
    body1 = resp1.json()

    resp2 = api_client.patch(f"/alerts/{alert_id}", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp2.status_code == 200
    body2 = resp2.json()

    assert body1 == body2

    get_resp = api_client.get(f"/alerts/{alert_id}")
    assert get_resp.json()["status"] == "closed"

tests.name: test_response_to_expired_alert_returns_410
preconditions: A fraud alert exists that was created more than 24 hours ago and has not been acknowledged
The system has auto-frozen the account associated with that alert
actions: PATCH /alerts/{id} with body {"response": "confirm"} and header Idempotency-Key: <unique_key> targeting the expired alert
expected: Response body contains an error code indicating the alert has expired
trace: R-FIN-088: error pathway - 410 when alert expired
tests.executable:
def test_response_to_expired_alert_returns_410(api_client, expired_fraud_alert, unique_idempotency_key):
    alert_id = expired_fraud_alert["id"]
    headers = {"Idempotency-Key": unique_idempotency_key}
    payload = {"response": "confirm"}

    resp = api_client.patch(f"/alerts/{alert_id}", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 410
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body
    assert body["error"].get("code") in ("ALERT_EXPIRED", "GONE", "alert_expired")

    account_id = expired_fraud_alert["account_id"]
    account_resp = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}")
    assert account_resp.json()["status"] == "frozen"

tests.name: test_unacknowledged_alert_auto_freezes_account_at_24h_boundary
preconditions: A fraud alert exists with created_at set to exactly 24 hours ago (boundary instant)
The alert has NOT been acknowledged by the customer
The system's scheduled freeze job has run at or after the 24-hour mark
actions: Wait for or simulate the system freeze job execution at T+24h
expected: Alert status is auto_frozen or closed_unacknowledged
trace: R-FIN-088: unacknowledged alerts shall auto-freeze the account; 24-hour boundary
tests.executable:
def test_unacknowledged_alert_auto_freezes_account_at_24h_boundary(
    api_client, alert_at_24h_boundary, unique_idempotency_key
):
    alert_id = alert_at_24h_boundary["id"]
    account_id = alert_at_24h_boundary["account_id"]

    <trigger_freeze_job>(api_client, alert_id)

    alert_resp = api_client.get(f"/alerts/{alert_id}")
    assert alert_resp.status_code == 200
    assert alert_resp.json()["status"] in ("auto_frozen", "closed_unacknowledged")

    account_resp = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}")
    assert account_resp.status_code == 200
    assert account_resp.json()["status"] == "frozen"

    patch_resp = api_client.patch(
        f"/alerts/{alert_id}",
        json={"response": "confirm"},
        headers={"Idempotency-Key": unique_idempotency_key},
    )
    assert patch_resp.status_code == 410
